title: Xtra Crisp Canadian Pea Eggplant Stew
description: A rich and comforting Canadian dish featuring pea-eggplant, cooked to perfection in a tandoor clay pot. This stew is crisp, delicious, and nut-free friendly.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor clay pot by filling it with cold water and letting it sit for 30 minutes.
Drain the water from the tandoor clay pot and add the olive oil, heating it over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the pea-eggplant, carrots, and potatoes, cooking for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the vegetable broth, salt, and black pepper, stirring to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
While the stew is cooking, prepare an ice bath by filling a large bowl with ice and water.
After the stew has cooked for 20 minutes, remove the tandoor clay pot from the heat and carefully place it in the ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Let the stew cool to room temperature, then refrigerate it until chilled.
Serve the stew chilled, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
